text.title: VPAT / ACR Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding vpat-acr-template. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: ACR MUST use VPAT 2.5 (ITI 2024) or later. Earlier editions (2.4 and below) lack WCAG 2.2 rows.
rule.rationale: Older editions cannot represent WCAG 2.2 SC; using them produces inaccurate conformance claims.

rule.statement: Each row's status MUST be one of: Supports / Partially Supports / Does Not Support / Not Applicable. Custom statuses are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Procurement teams parse the four-option set; custom statuses break their tooling.

rule.statement: Any status other than 'Supports' MUST include remarks naming the specific limitation and the remediation plan or accommodation.
rule.rationale: Bare 'Partially Supports' without context is unactionable for the procurement reviewer.

rule.statement: Every row MUST cite the evaluation method (automated, manual, AT testing, code review). Unnamed evaluation = unaudited claim.
rule.rationale: Evaluation method is what makes the claim verifiable; without it the ACR is marketing copy.

rule.statement: ACR MUST be signed by a named accessibility lead with role + date. Unsigned ACRs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous ACRs are rejected by procurement; named ownership is part of the evidence.
